ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Kernel functions for diverse types of data, including but not restricted to nonnegative and real vectors, real matrices, categorical and ordinal variables, sets, and strings, plus utilities like kernel similarity, kernel principal components analysis (PCA), and feature importance for support vector machines (SVMs), which expand other 'R' packages like 'kernlab'.

Kernel functions for diverse types of data, including but not restricted to nonnegative and real vectors, real matrices, categorical and ordinal variables, sets, and strings, plus utilities like kernel similarity, kernel principal components analysis (PCA), and feature importance for support vector machines (SVMs), which expand other 'R' packages like 'kernlab'.

name: r-kerntools
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '1.2.1' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-kerntools
  version: "{{ version|replace('-', '_') }}"

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/kerntools_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/kerntools/kerntools_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 47b74d5d413a734069f22a72c97372415a66ff9a3f4e2bd871665f8d2e9956bd

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-dplyr
    - r-ggplot2
    - r-kernlab
    - r-reshape2
    - r-stringi
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-dplyr
    - r-ggplot2
    - r-kernlab
    - r-reshape2
    - r-stringi

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('kerntools')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('kerntools')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://github.com/elies-ramon/kerntools
  doc_url: https://elies-ramon.github.io/kerntools/
  license: GPL-3.0-only
  summary: Kernel functions for diverse types of data, including but not restricted
    to nonnegative and real vectors, real matrices, categorical and ordinal variables,
    sets, and strings, plus utilities like kernel similarity, kernel principal components
    analysis (PCA), and feature importance for support vector machines (SVMs), which
    expand other 'R' packages like 'kernlab'.
  license_family: GPL3
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/GPL-3'

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: kerntools
# Type: Package
# Title: Kernel Functions and Tools for Machine Learning Applications
# Version: 1.2.1
# Authors@R: person("Elies", "Ramon", email = "eramon@everlyrusher.com", role = c("aut","cre","cph"),  comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-7953-8115"))
# Description: Kernel functions for diverse types of data (including, but not restricted to: nonnegative and real vectors, real matrices, categorical and ordinal variables, sets, strings), plus other utilities like kernel similarity, kernel Principal Components Analysis (PCA) and features' importance for Support Vector Machines (SVMs), which expand other 'R' packages like 'kernlab'.
